decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with cards like Consider and Sleight of Hand to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard. Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse provide removal options while Picklock Prankster allows for card advantage and disruption. Treasure Cruise helps refuel the hand, while Opt and Artist's Talent provide card selection. The deck aims to be fast and aggressive, using efficient spells like Into the Flood Maw and Cori-Steel Cutter to push for damage quickly. The inclusion of Profit's Eidetic Memory and Hall of Storm Giants offer additional late-game advantage and finishers.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo pieces, using cards like Formidable Speaker and Witherbloom Command to support the strategy. It also includes disruption like Thoughtseize and Cache Grab to control the game. The deck aims to create a strong board presence with cards like Esika's Chariot and Parhelion II, while also including powerful finishers such as Skysovereign, Consul Flagship and Overlord of the Balemurk. The combination of combo pieces, disruption, and strong threats makes for a versatile and formidable deck in Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence with powerful legendary creatures and utilizing synergies between them. The goal is to apply pressure early on with efficient creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan with control elements like Darkslick Shores and Honest Rutstein. By leveraging the diverse array of legendary creatures available in a five-color deck, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. Additionally, the inclusion of Mox Amber and Relic of Legends helps accelerate the deck's game plan by providing mana ramp and card advantage.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells. The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with cards like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves to cast powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. Collected Company provides additional creatures to the board while cards like Enduring Innocence and Dromoka's Command help to control the opponent's board state. The deck aims to be aggressive and proactive in order to put pressure on the opponent and secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo by using cards like Thoughtseize and Cache Grab to control the opponent's hand and resources. Other cards like Esika's Chariot and Formidable Speaker help to generate tokens and create battlefield presence. Parhelion II is the key card in the combo, which can be supported by cards like Skysovereign, Consul Flagship to deal damage and secure victory. The deck also includes some land cards like Godless Shrine and Concealed Courtyard to ensure mana consistency for the combo to go off smoothly.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly take control of the board and deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. The deck utilizes efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy. By utilizing cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Emberheart Challenger, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain tempo throughout the game. Additionally, cards like Boomerang Basics and Academic Dispute provide disruption and card advantage to keep the opponent on their toes.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells, removal, and card advantage provided by Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, Omen of the Sea, and Consult the Star Charts. The main win condition is grinding out the opponent with card advantage and controlling the board until the player achieves victory. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional card advantage and creates a more value-oriented game plan. The mana base is built to ensure consistent access to the deck's powerful spells and abilities.

This UW Control deck focuses on card advantage and controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card draw. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a dominant position with powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. It also utilizes cards like Consult the Star Charts and Stock Up to maintain a steady flow of resources while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Get Lost and Dovin's Veto. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win through incremental advantages.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate incredible value and overwhelming board presence. Utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Cache Grab to disrupt the opponent's strategy while ramping into key pieces like Overlord of the Balemurk and Esika's Chariot. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of token generation, powerful creatures, and disruptive elements to secure victory.

The focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. By utilizing powerful control cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Supreme Verdict, along with card advantage engines like Meticulous Archive and Consult the Star Charts, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast its opponents. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion provides additional support and synergy for the deck's overall game plan. Overall, the key concept of this UW Control deck is to efficiently control the game and grind out victories through resource management and powerful threats.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing a variety of cards that disrupt the opponent's strategy and gain incremental advantages. Cards like Authority of the Consuls and Caretaker's Talent help to slow down the opponent, while cards like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Ossification provide strong win conditions. The deck also includes a companion, Yorion, Sky Nomad, to provide added value and flexibility. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and establish dominance through careful resource management and strategic play.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble and protect Lotus Field, a key combo piece that can generate large amounts of mana. By using cards like Scapeshift and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods, the deck aims to ramp up its mana production and set up powerful plays. With support cards like Crumbling Vestige and Arboreal Grazer, the deck can accelerate its game plan and overwhelm opponents with its mana advantage. The deck also includes additional combo pieces like Formidable Speaker and Echoing Deeps to further enhance its synergy and consistency in executing its game plan.

The main focus of this Pioneer 1 vs 1 Aggro Red Deck Wins deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells. By using cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger, alongside efficient burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Additionally, with cards like Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Reckless Rage, the deck can maintain pressure on the opponent while dealing with threats on the board. Overall, the deck prioritizes fast and aggressive gameplay to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on aggressive play, utilizing fast and efficient creatures like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger to quickly burn through the opponent's life total. With spells like Monstrous Rage, Reckless Rage, and Lightning Strike, the deck aims to control the battlefield and deal direct damage to the opponent. The inclusion of Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provides additional sources of damage to close out games swiftly. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature aggression and burn spells.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful combo using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. It includes a variety of cards to support and protect the combo, such as Formidable Speaker, Witherbloom Command, and Thoughtseize. The deck aims to control the board and set up the combo pieces quickly to achieve victory. Additionally, it includes cards like Concealed Courtyard and Bitter Triumph to provide additional support and utility throughout the game.

This deck aims to assemble the combo of Lotus Field, Scapeshift, and Thassa's Oracle to win the game. It utilizes cards like Arboreal Grazer and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods to ramp up mana quickly and protect the combo pieces. The deck also includes cards like Aftermath Analyst and The Wandering Minstrel to filter through the deck and find the necessary pieces. With the ability to generate a large amount of mana and manipulate the top of the library, this deck can quickly and efficiently win the game through its combo.
